questions.text: Critical CVE has exploit in the wild; patch available. Servers are 30 days behind. FIRST priority?

options.text: Disable patching to avoid reboots, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Patch printers first always, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Internet-facing and vulnerable services before internal file shares
Wait for annual maintenance window only, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Risk-based prioritization focuses on exposure and exploitability.
questions.concept: Prioritize known-exploited vulns; emergency change beats waiting for quarterly cycles.

questions.text: Test environment lacks patches 'because it is not prod.' Risk?

options.text: Test networks cannot be reached ever, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Attackers pivot from lab to prod via shared credentials and trusts
Patches break coffee machines only, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
CVEs do not apply to VMs, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Unpatched labs are common pivot points.
questions.concept: Prioritize known-exploited vulns; emergency change beats waiting for quarterly cycles.

questions.text: WSUS shows 95% compliance but Nessus still flags missing KBs. Likely cause?

options.text: Nessus invents CVEs, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Superseded updates not installed or wrong scan credentials / reboot pending
Compliance is always 100% accurate, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Patches remove antivirus, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Reconcile WSUS agent state with vuln scanner and pending reboots.
questions.concept: Prioritize known-exploited vulns; emergency change beats waiting for quarterly cycles.

questions.text: CISA adds a VPN vendor CVE to KEV; your appliance is two versions behind. Priority?

options.text: Disable automatic updates on the VPN to prevent unexpected reboots during holidays, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Emergency patch or isolate VPN per vendor advisory within hours, not next quarter
Publish the CVE internally but defer patching until budget approval next year, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Wait for the next penetration test to validate exploitability in your environment, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Known exploited vulnerabilities on edge VPNs are top patching priority.
questions.concept: Prioritize known-exploited vulns; emergency change beats waiting for quarterly cycles.

questions.text: Inventory finds Java 8 on 400 endpoints for one legacy app. BEST approach?

options.text: Ignore because Java is popular, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable Windows Update instead, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Uninstall Java on all machines including servers needed, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Isolate app in controlled network segment and plan upgrade or virtualization wrapper

questions.explanation: Compensating controls plus migration beat ignoring EOL runtimes.
questions.concept: EOL software lacks patches—segment, compensate, or retire with documented risk.

questions.text: Browser extensions with broad permissions haven't updated in two years. Risk?

options.text: HIPAA bans browsers, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Abandoned extensions become supply-chain targets
Extensions cannot access tabs, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Only mobile apps matter, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Stale extensions are a growing endpoint risk.
questions.concept: EOL software lacks patches—segment, compensate, or retire with documented risk.

questions.text: SCADA systems run Windows 7 embedded. Security recommendation?

options.text: Share one admin password, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Connect SCADA to guest Wi-Fi, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Network segmentation, monitoring, and documented exception with compensating controls
Install consumer antivirus only and expose RDP, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Unsupported OS needs isolation and monitoring when upgrade is delayed.
questions.concept: EOL software lacks patches—segment, compensate, or retire with documented risk.

questions.text: A critical app requires Java 8 with no vendor roadmap; server sits in the DMZ. Mitigation?

options.text: Install additional antivirus products until three vendors report clean scans, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Segment heavily, monitor closely, and fund migration with documented risk acceptance
Move the server to the internal LAN without segmentation to reduce latency
Disable TLS on the app to improve performance for external customers, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Unsupported runtimes need network isolation and a retirement plan.
questions.concept: EOL software lacks patches—segment, compensate, or retire with documented risk.

questions.text: Terraform plan shows manual firewall holes on prod not in code. What does this indicate?

options.text: Faster MFA enrollment, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Improved security posture, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Automatic HIPAA compliance, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Configuration drift undermining infrastructure as code guarantees

questions.explanation: Manual changes bypass review; drift detection and import/remediation needed.
questions.concept: Baseline configuration and IaC prevent silent weakening of security controls.

questions.text: CIS benchmark scan: SSH PermitRootLogin yes on new cloud VMs. Fix?

options.text: Document as acceptable forever, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Share root password in chat, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Enforce golden image / cloud-init baseline and continuous compliance scanning
Disable SSH and use no remote access ever, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Baselines and scanning prevent redeploy drift.
questions.concept: Baseline configuration and IaC prevent silent weakening of security controls.

questions.text: AD GPO reports different password policy on one OU. Likely impact?

options.text: No security effect, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Stronger encryption on Wi-Fi, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Weaker credentials on accounts in that OU attract spray attacks
Automatic patch compliance, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Policy inconsistency creates weak pockets attackers target.
questions.concept: Baseline configuration and IaC prevent silent weakening of security controls.

questions.text: CSPM reports public SSH open on a prod instance that Terraform should manage privately. Fix?

options.text: Revert drift via IaC pipeline, investigate manual change, and enforce policy-as-code guardrails
Add the security group rule to the template so drift becomes permanent, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable CSPM because Terraform already ran successfully last year, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Snapshot the instance and advertise SSH in the security newsletter as a feature

questions.explanation: Drift from IaC baselines should be corrected and prevented with guardrails.
questions.concept: Baseline configuration and IaC prevent silent weakening of security controls.

questions.text: MDM shows 15% iOS devices below minimum OS. Enforcement action?

options.text: Allow indefinitely with warning email, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Block corporate email and VPN until compliant or quarantined VLAN
Wipe all devices immediately without notice, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable MDM to reduce tickets, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Conditional access drives mobile compliance.
questions.concept: Measure patch SLA adherence; exceptions need risk acceptance and expiry.

questions.text: Servers miss updates because change freeze lasts six months. Risk acceptance should note?

options.text: Freezes guarantee zero incidents, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Attackers respect change freezes, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Updates are cosmetic only, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Accumulated critical CVE exposure for entire freeze period

questions.explanation: Long freezes need explicit risk sign-off and compensating monitoring.
questions.concept: Measure patch SLA adherence; exceptions need risk acceptance and expiry.

questions.text: Which metric BEST measures update compliance?

options.text: CCTV camera count, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Email volume per user, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Number of help desk tickets about fonts, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
% assets at or above mandated patch/OS baseline within SLA

questions.explanation: Baseline adherence percentage is a standard compliance KPI.
questions.concept: Measure patch SLA adherence; exceptions need risk acceptance and expiry.

questions.text: Only 62% of laptops meet the 14-day critical patch SLA. Best program improvement?

options.text: Extend SLA to one year for all devices to achieve one hundred percent compliance
Stop measuring compliance because users dislike reboot notifications, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Report by business unit, enforce maintenance windows, and escalate chronic noncompliance
Allow users to opt out of updates if they sign a paper waiver, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Metrics and accountability drive patch compliance, not indefinite SLAs.
questions.concept: Measure patch SLA adherence; exceptions need risk acceptance and expiry.

questions.text: Vendor ends support for your firewall OS next month. Priority?

options.text: Expose management UI to internet, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Plan upgrade or replacement before EOS—no new CVE patches after
Buy extended support from eBay, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable logging to hide issues, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: EOS means unpatched vulnerabilities over time.
questions.concept: Unsupported OS on critical networks requires segmentation and explicit risk acceptance.

questions.text: Windows Server 2012 R2 remains for one app. Compensating control?

Stop backups to save money, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Isolate segment, deny internet egress, monitor closely, fund migration
Enable RDP from any IP, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Unsupported servers need network containment and migration plan.

Connect devices to guest Wi-Fi for updates, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Assume HIPAA exempts all devices, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

options.text: Disable encryption on device traffic to simplify legacy protocol troubleshooting, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
